drama.objectives: force private grief into an irreversible public reading
drama.conflictLines: the protagonist tries to hold tenderness and truth together while grief and institutions keep separating them

payoffs.default: the chapter exits with a more exposed wound and less shelter from consequence
payoffs.final: the chapter delivers a truth that repairs some bonds only by breaking older arrangements

detective-police.objectives: lock the investigation to evidence that the system cannot safely absorb
detective-police.conflictLines: the protagonist follows evidence while every official response attempts to redirect, delay, or domesticate it

payoffs.default: the chapter exits with cleaner evidence and dirtier institutional pressure
payoffs.final: the chapter delivers accusation, proof, and public fallout in the same movement

science-fiction.objectives: push memory control into a choice between safety and freedom
science-fiction.conflictLines: the protagonist tries to restore human agency while the system reframes freedom as a security defect

payoffs.default: the chapter exits with sharper ethical visibility and reduced procedural safety
payoffs.final: the chapter delivers a civic choice that redefines what survival means

fantasy.objectives: bring the inherited curse into a truth-bound confrontation
fantasy.conflictLines: the protagonist seeks truthful release while inherited power keeps demanding silence as the price of belonging

payoffs.default: the chapter exits with greater magical clarity and heavier cost
payoffs.final: the chapter delivers sacrifice and release without restoring the old order

romance-relational.objectives: turn collaboration into a test of honest intimacy
romance-relational.conflictLines: the protagonist wants genuine partnership while habit keeps translating vulnerability into negotiation

payoffs.default: the chapter exits with greater intimacy pressure and less emotional cover
payoffs.final: the chapter delivers commitment that remains honest about risk
